lesson.menu_desc: Comparatives: describing your surgical team -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 8 -- Describing People and Teams -- comparatives
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Andreia Heins | Aula 8 -- Describing People and Teams

listenings.text: Hello Andreia, this is David Lewis again. For the training program, I would like to know your surgical team better. Could you describe the people you work with? Tell me who is more experienced, who is more skilled with the robotic system, and who stays calmer under pressure. I am very interested in their strengths. For example, is your scrub nurse more detail-oriented than the others? Is your youngest resident more confident now than before? A reliable and supportive team is the most important thing for this program. Please prepare a short profile of three colleagues. Compare them, and tell me what makes each one special. This will help us allocate the right mentors. Thank you, Andreia, and talk to you soon.

Good morning. Let me introduce my surgical team at the hospital. First, Mariana, my scrub nurse. She is the most detail-oriented person I know. She checks every instrument twice, and she is more reliable than anyone on the floor. Then there is Pedro, our youngest resident. He is less experienced than the rest of us, but he is incredibly hardworking, and he is more confident every week. Carla, our anesthesiologist, is the calmest member of the team. In an emergency, nobody stays calmer than her. I am more efficient when I work with these three people, because they are supportive and easygoing. Of course, I am still learning. Some colleagues are more skilled than me with the new robotic arms, and that is good, because I learn from them every day. Together, we are stronger than any of us alone.

extra_audio.text: Let me rank my team by experience. First, Carla is the most experienced surgeon in the room. Then I come next, with twelve years in the operating room. Mariana, our scrub nurse, has worked here for six years. Pedro, the resident, is newer, but he learns faster than anyone. And our intern, Lucas, started last month, so he is the least experienced of all.
